title: Financial Literacy Training * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Financial Literacy Training * Native American Community 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Ex-Offenders Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER MICRO ENTERPRISE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance, training in business skills, assistance writing business plans, access to financial business counseling, individual development accounts (IDAs), and other support for individuals who want to establish a small business. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as tribal members Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific homeownership coaching, education, and programming to help people buy and keep their homes, increase homeownership opportunities for Portland's Urban Native Community, and close the Native homeownership gap. Also offers financial education classes. NAYA is a HUD-approved counseling agency and an equal housing opportunity provider. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Unrestricted, primarily serving self-identified Native Americans Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 288-8177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER MICRO ENTERPRISE
